text: The surgeon has completed the release of the gastric fundus, reflecting the surrounding tissue to expose the surgical zone.
The surgeon completes the myotomy, extending five centimeters towards the gastric reservoir. A partial wrap is then performed around the stomach.
The surgeon completes the first layer of suturing, with the fundus visibly positioned and secured in place.
The surgeon visually inspects the left aspect of the myotomy, focusing on the lens of the myotomy. The right border of the myotomy is also examined, marking the completion of the surgical line.
The surgeon completes the partial wrap around the gastric reservoir, securing the fundus in place.
